wire_delay_model.links_note: uniform 8x8 grid on 20mm x 20mm die; tile pitch a = 2.5 mm; all 112 cardinal links = 1 cycle (225 ps wire / 250 ps period). Full per-link enumeration generated by /tmp/hexa-arch-rfc001-measurement/runs/gen_topologies.py; not inlined.

verdict.rationale: single-topology record; F1/F2 verdict requires the pair-verdict aggregator over this + the degree-6 hex mate at identical (traffic, node, wire_delay_model)

wire_delay_source.node_basis: SMART 22nm TSMC GP, characterization Table 2; reaffirmed OpenSMART ISPASS 2017 §III

leighton_source.edition: Leighton 1984 + Leighton 1992 §1.4; Dally & Towles PPIN 2004 §3.2-3.3 mesh closed forms

scope_caveats: single (clock, node, placement) point — not a regime claim
axial-hex on square grid same-sign pair diameter = mesh diameter; degree-6 advantage only helps opposite-sign pairs
4 GHz is the first integer-cycle frequency where diagonal links cost 2 cycles; clock sweep not performed
22nm SMART is the most-modern public characterization; newer-FinFET sensitivity not performed
